user_message: Sarah Wilson at Litware Inc just sent this. Help me investigate the decline.

Meeting: escalation triage Discussion - Litware Inc

Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc), David Chen (Microsoft)

[00:01:15] Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc): Appreciate everyone making time. The main agenda today is escalation triage.

[00:07:00] Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is technical validation.

[00:11:00] David Chen (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is vendor dependencies.

[00:13:00] Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc): On the escalation triage front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around integration complexity.

[00:16:00] David Chen (Microsoft): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about stakeholder alignment. Can we address those?

[00:20:00] David Chen (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is vendor dependencies.

[00:25:00] Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc): Let me add some context -- we've been working on security compliance and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

[00:29:00] Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc): The timeline for performance requirements is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:34:00] Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc): Let me add some context -- we've been working on performance requirements and found that the current vendor contract has a 90-day exit clause we need to plan around.

[00:37:00] David Chen (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on integration complexity and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

[00:40:00] Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc): On the escalation triage front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around budget approval.
